channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hearing looks at rules for evidence and other details in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Prosecutors and attorneys for a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students in 2022 argued some of the final ground rules they want for Bryan Kohberger's trial in a hearing Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republican bill requiring proof of citizenship for voting passes US House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House passed one of Republicans' signature issues for the year on Thursday, approving legislation to require proof of U.S. citizenship when registering to vote for federal elections, one of President Donald Trump's top election-related priorities.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, CHRISTINE FERNANDO and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pressed for evidence against Mahmoud Khalil, government cites its power to deport people for beliefs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Facing a deadline from an immigration judge to turn over evidence for its attempted deportation of Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil, the federal government has instead submitted a brief memo, signed by Secretary of State Marco Rubio, citing the Trump administration’s autho
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The Latest: House narrowly passes framework for Trump’s big spending bill
item.description: House Republicans narrowly approved their budget framework on Thursday, a political turnaround after Speaker Mike Johnson worked into the night to satisfy GOP holdouts who had refused to advance trillions of dollars in tax breaks without deeper spending cuts. U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Emaarion Boyd steals 6 bases without a hit, 1st minor league player to do it since at least 2005
item.description: CEDAR RAPIDS, Iowa (AP) — Emaarion Boyd became the first minor league player in at least 20 years to steal six bases in a game without getting a hit when he pulled off the feat for High-A Beloit (Wisconsin) against Cedar Rapids.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Homeland Security agents denied entry at two Los Angeles schools
item.description: It is believed to be the first time federal agents tried to enter Los Angeles schools since President Trump announced a crackdown on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers say airplanes bumped each other on runway at Reagan National
item.description: Multiple lawmakers on Thursday said their airplane was bumped by another plane while waiting on the runway at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US ambassador to Ukraine to step down as peace talks uncertain
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. ambassador to Ukraine is stepping down from her post after nearly three years in Kyiv amid uncertainty over the Trump administration’s attempts to broker a peace deal to end the Russia-Ukraine war.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Florida brings charges against a man jailed on federal counts of trying to assassinate Trump
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — A man already jailed on federal charges of attempting to assassinate then-presidential candidate Donald Trump as he golfed last fall will face additional state terrorism and attempted murder charges, Florida's attorney general said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida charges Trump assassination attempt suspect with attempted murder, terrorism
item.description: Ryan Routh, the suspected would-be Trump assassin, faces Florida state charges of attempted murder and terrorism for his alleged plot to kill the president at his golf course.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Johnson presses ahead with vote on Trump budget blueprint
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said he was pressing forward with a vote Thursday morning on the party's “one big, beautiful bill” full of President Trump’s legislative agenda, holding a joint appearance with Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9

item.title: Author Jillian Lauren, wife of Weezer bassist, arrested after being shot by police
item.description: Jillian Lauren, author and wife of Weezer bassist Scott Shriner, was shot and injured by Los Angeles police and arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after pointing a gun at officers from her front yard, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: White House Christmas ornament honors 150 years of culinary diplomacy practiced at state dinners
item.description: This year's official White House Christmas ornament honors more than 150 years of culinary diplomacy practiced at state dinners.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Johnson scraps vote on Trump budget blueprint in face of conservative opposition
item.description: House Republican leaders on Wednesday scrapped plans to vote on the Senate’s framework to advance key parts of President Trump’s legislative agenda, a major setback that came in the face of opposition from hard-line conservatives. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Students react to nearly 50 international students' visas being revoked, records being removed across Utah
item.description: International students at institutions across Utah and around the country are having their records terminated or their visas revoked. Here's what some students said about it.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Sunny and pleasant afternoon, with warmer temperatures to come
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) - Happy Wednesday, Utah! High pressure is slowly building in from the west and taking over our weather pattern. The high pressure will not only bring dry weather to the forecast but also come with the return of even warmer temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Alana Brophy, ABC 4

item.title: Shark attack survivor presses her state for an alert system to keep people safe in the water
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama teen who narrowly survived a fierce shark attack last year said she hopes a proposed alert system before state lawmakers can help keep others safe in the water.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US Postal Service seeks to hike cost of a first-class stamp
item.description: If approved, the increase for a “forever” stamp and similar increases for postcards, metered letters and international mail would take effect July 13.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration appeals court decision ordering AP journalists back into White House events
item.description: The Trump administration said Wednesday it would appeal a federal court decision that ordered it to re-admit Associated Press journalists to White House events on First Amendment grounds.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, except China
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, except China
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Didn't get a Trader Joe's mini pastel tote? You can buy one for over $1,000 on eBay
item.description: And as they did last year, customers were quick to snag the bags. If you weren't among them, you're in luck — but only if you're willing to spend a pretty penny.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KRON4

item.title: Are dire wolves de-extinct? Genetically engineered pups spark controversy
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: LeBron James becomes first pro male athlete to have likeness depicted in Ken doll
item.description: LeBron James has become the first professional male athlete to have his likeness depicted in a Ken doll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: YouTuber and entrepreneur MrBeast slams Trump's 'brutal' tariffs
item.description: MrBeast, whose real name is Jimmy Donaldson, called the tariffs a "nail in the coffin" for small businesses.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Group calls for investigation into Ms. Rachel over posts about Gaza children
item.description: Rachel Griffin Accurso, who goes by Ms. Rachel to her more than 14 million subscribers on YouTube, makes videos for children.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wall Street surges to one of its best days since WWII after Trump pauses most tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks soared to one of their best days in history on a euphoric Wall Street Wednesday after President Donald Trump said he would back off on most of his tariffs temporarily, as investors had so desperately hoped he would.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Wall Street surges to one of its best days since WWII after Trump pauses most tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks soared to one of their best days in history on a euphoric Wall Street Wednesday after President Donald Trump said he would back off on most of his tariffs temporarily, as investors had so desperately hoped he would.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump to fine migrants up to $1K daily for defying deportation order
item.description: The administration is planning to retroactively apply the fines for up to five years, which means some immigrants could owe more than $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, Fox 5

item.title: New Mexico governor mobilizes National Guard to tackle crime emergency in Albuquerque
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham on Tuesday declared a state of emergency in New Mexico’s largest city, saying that a significant increase in crime in Albuquerque warrants the help of the New Mexico National Guard.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration freezes $1.8 billion for Cornell and Northwestern
item.description: Cornell University and Northwestern University say they haven't received any notice from the U.S. government about paused federal funding even though the White House says more than $1 billion for Cornell and about $790 million for Northwestern have been frozen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Microsoft fires employees who protested its support for Israel at 50th anniversary event
item.description: Two Microsoft employees were fired for protests condemning the company’s support for Israel at a 50th anniversary event last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4
